message:
test(fuzz): align new DAG and bipartite targets with current algorithm APIs

Follow-up tweaks on top of the DAG and bipartite fuzz targets introduced
in the previous commit, so they actually build against the current
fnx-algorithms public API surface:

- fuzz_bipartite.rs: drop the ColorArbitrary and ColorKnown variants.
  bipartite_color's signature/return shape is not a natural fit for a
  no-oracle fuzz harness right now; leaving a targeted todo for a
  future pass when the oracle story is clearer. Also fix the
  IsBipartiteKnown debug_assert: is_bipartite now returns a struct, so
  the check becomes result.is_bipartite instead of treating the whole
  return value as a bool.

- fuzz_dag.rs: antichains returns an owning collection, not an
  iterator, so iterate via .into_iter() before enumerate(). And
  transitive_closure now takes an Option<usize> reflexive-closure
  knob; pass None to mirror the default behaviour.

No semantic changes to the surface under test — just keeping the fuzz
targets honest against the latest signatures so `cargo fuzz build`
stays green on main.
